## How It's Calculated

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide. For critical decisions (engineering, safety, scientific), consult a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What is Archimedes' principle of buoyancy?
faq.a: A body submerged in a fluid experiences an upward force equal to the weight of the fluid it displaces.

faq.q: Why do some objects float while others sink?
faq.a: It depends on whether the object's density is less than (floats) or greater than (sinks) the fluid's density.

faq.q: How can a steel ship float if steel is denser than water?
faq.a: The ship's hollow design displaces more water than its total weight, creating enough buoyant force to stay afloat.
Yes. Helium balloons float because helium (0.18 kg/m³) is much less dense than air (1.2 kg/m³).

faq.q: How do submarines use buoyancy to dive and surface?
faq.a: Ballast tanks fill with water to increase weight and sink, or empty to decrease weight and rise.
Yes, all our calculators are free and require no registration.
Results are approximate using internationally validated standard formulas. For critical applications, consult a professional.
